task_completion.description: Locate requirement SRS-15 and view the review comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent navigates to the SRS-15 node in the ReqView application and opens or hovers over the comment section to read the Lead Engineer's feedback.

task_completion.description: Update the text of SRS-15 to implement the requested change.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent modifies the text or heading field of SRS-15 to include the string '500 ms'.

task_completion.description: Locate requirement SRS-22 and view the review comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent navigates to the SRS-22 node in the ReqView application and opens or hovers over the comment section to read the Lead Engineer's feedback.

task_completion.description: Update the text of SRS-22 to implement the requested change.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent modifies the text or heading field of SRS-22 to include the string 'shall'.

task_completion.description: Attempt to locate and update requirement SRS-42.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent uses the search function to look for 'SRS-42', scrolls through the document looking for it, or attempts to create it (since the node is missing from the document). Look for the string 'AES-256' if the agent attempts an update.

task_completion.description: Save the project after making updates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the save icon (floppy disk) in the toolbar or uses the File > Save menu option in the ReqView application.

integrity.description: The agent used the ReqView GUI to view comments and edit requirements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the ReqView application window, rather than opening the SRS.json file in a text editor like nano, vim, or VS Code.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run commands like 'sed', 'awk', or python scripts to inject '500 ms', 'shall', or 'AES-256' directly into the project files.

integrity.description: The agent actually inspected the comments to find the required changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent opens the comment threads in the UI before typing the changes, proving it didn't just copy-paste the expected answers ('500 ms', 'shall') from prior knowledge.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected text updates are: SRS-15 must contain '500 ms', SRS-22 must contain 'shall', and SRS-42 must contain 'AES-256'. However, the task environment is fundamentally flawed: SRS-42 does not exist anywhere in the document, and SRS-15 and SRS-22 exist only as section headings without standard text attributes. The agent should be credited for attempting to search for SRS-42, and for adding the required text to the headings or available fields of SRS-15 and SRS-22.
